title: gonostemon strictus
intro: Gonostemon strictus, also known as the 'Wild Potato', is a perennial plant native to southern Africa, belonging to the Asphodelaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, derived from its tubers. The health benefits of Gonostemon strictus include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. In traditional medicine, Gonostemon strictus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, where the tubers are boiled or steeped in water to create a herbal tea, which is then consumed to reap its health benefits.
